description: Decision rules and heuristics for agent memory management

duplicateDetection.description: Files with >60% overlap in content are likely duplicates
duplicateDetection.consolidation_rule: Keep file if it is comprehensive superset of other. Archive if redundant.

critical_facts.description: Time-sensitive facts that change frequently; verify weekly

normal_facts.description: Facts that change less frequently; check monthly

historical_facts.description: Lessons and patterns that rarely change; review quarterly

semanticMapping.description: Map task context to relevant memory files
semanticMapping.method: Extract task keywords from user request → search MEMORY.md for matching files by description and metadata

usageTracking.description: Track which files solve which problems (feedback loops)
outcomes: irrelevant - memory not useful for this task

criteria: Only consolidate if one file is comprehensive superset of other
If files are complementary (different purposes), keep separate
Archive old file, don't delete (preserve in history)
Always verify consolidation with user before executing

conditions: Incident is resolved and no longer actionable
Information is superseded by newer memory

metrics.duplicate_count: Number of duplicate file pairs detected
metrics.usage_quality_score: Average success rate across all files (0-100, target 95%+)
metrics.consolidation_opportunities: Number of duplicate pairs that could be consolidated
metrics.semantic_link_coverage: Number of task types mapped to memory files

confidenceRules.description: When to be confident vs cautious in recommendations
confidenceRules.high_confidence_consolidation: Files >70% similar AND one is clear superset
confidenceRules.low_confidence: Files >60% similar but serve different purposes (keep both)
confidenceRules.when_unsure: Ask user: 'These files overlap. Consolidate or keep separate?'

indexRules.description: Rules for maintaining MEMORY.md index quality
indexRules.index_is_source_of_truth: If a file exists but isn't in MEMORY.md, update the index
indexRules.drift_detection: Index drift = file exists in disk but missing from MEMORY.md, or vice versa
indexRules.entry_format: One line per file: '[Name](file.md) — one-line description (~150 chars max)'
indexRules.categories: Organize by semantic groups (Infrastructure & Operations, Feedback & Rules, Systems & Processes, etc.)
indexRules.task_mapping: Include 'When to Load What' section at top for quick navigation
indexRules.stale_mapping: If index mapping is wrong (task X should load Y but doesn't), flag for update
indexRules.add_files_to_index: Any new memory file must be added to MEMORY.md within same session as creation

evolution.feedback_loop: After each audit/update, log what worked/didn't work
evolution.refinement: Over time, adjust thresholds based on false positives/negatives
evolution.index_improvement: Track if agent found files quickly via MEMORY.md or had to search
evolution.proposal_opportunity: If index frequently misses files for a task type, add new mapping
